name: withania somnifera (ashwagandha)
mechanism: Human evidence suggests modulation of hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal stress signaling and inflammatory mediators.
interactions: Potential additive sedation with CNS depressants
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated. May cause somnolence, gastrointestinal upset or headache. Avoid in pregnancy; Use caution with thyroid-active therapy; Use caution in autoimmune disease or with immunosuppressive therapy; Avoid combining with sedatives, generally well tolerated; Avoid in pregnancy, somnolence, gastrointestinal upset or headache; Avoid combining with sedatives.
description: Withania somnifera (ashwagandha) is an adaptogenic herb studied mainly for stress and anxiety symptom reduction in standardized extracts.
dosage: Common adult use is 300-600 mg/day of root extract standardized to withanolides.
